title: Powerhouse uses document-model-driven architecture where document models are the core reusable unit enabling rapid application development
content: Powerhouse's architecture centers on document models as structured representations of data and workflows that evolve over time through operations. The three-layer architecture consists of: (1) Data Infrastructure Layer with the Reactor for storage/sync across local, cloud, and decentralized systems; (2) Host Application Layer with Connect, Switchboard, Fusion, Renown, and Academy as modular host apps gaining functionality through plugins; (3) Customization Layer for organization-specific plugins. Document models enable asynchronous collaboration (contributors work without linear processes), customizable processes (unique workflows per team), and event-driven updates. Front-end developers can 'plug into any document model and start building UI components without the hassle of managing backend or state management' -- the event-driven architecture handles it, like Redux without setup.
description: Document models capture data, workflows, and operations as living entities; front-end devs can build UIs without managing backend state.
